$comment: Single source of truth for ship-conventions.sh hook. Edit here, not in the script.

branch.$comment: Branch naming is advisory — enforced by skill instructions, not hooks. User-provided names always take precedence.
branch.guidanceMessage: Recommended: Jira ticket (e.g., PROJ-123, PROJ-456-short-desc) or kebab-case (e.g., fix-auth-bug). Commit types (feat/, fix/) belong in commit messages, not branch names.

commit.processSubstitutionMessage: Never use $() or <() in git commit commands. Use the Write tool to create docs/<identifier>/commit-msg.txt, then run: git commit -F docs/<identifier>/commit-msg.txt
commit.featureBranchMessage: Never commit directly on main/master. Create a feature branch first (git checkout -b <branch-name>).

staging.blockMessage: Stage specific files by name (git add path/to/file1 path/to/file2). Never use git add -A, git add --all, or git add .
staging.blockedFileMessage: commit-msg*.txt files are in .gitignore and must not be staged. They are throwaway build artifacts used only by git commit -F.

pr.draftMessage: PRs must be created as drafts. Add --draft flag to gh pr create.
